name: Color
comment:
An Elm package to work with web colors.

Example usage

    colors : List Color
    colors =
        [ hsl 194 0.49 0.14
        , fromHexUnsafe "#06A77D"
        , rgb 0.96 0.976 0.996
        , rgb255 122 137 194
        ]

    colorPalette : List Color
    colorPalette =
        fromPalette "https://coolors.co/40f99b-61707d"

    green : Color
    green =
        hsl 164 0.93 0.34

    fontColor : Color
    fontColor =
        if isLight green then
            black

        else
            white

    viewHtml : Html msg
    viewHtml =
        Html.div
            [ style "background-color" (Color.toCssString green)
            , style "color" (Color.toCssString fontColor)
            ]
            [ Html.text (toCssString green) ]

# Variants

# Helper colors

@docs black, white

aliases.name: Color
aliases.comment: The Color type representing a color in rgba space

values.name: fromHex
values.comment:
Parse a hex string to a color

Can handle different formats:

    fromHex "#000"

    fromHex "#F0F0F0"

    fromHex "0xA0A0A0"

    fromHex "8F8F8F"

    fromHex "#C6C6C680"

values.name: fromHexUnsafe
values.comment: Like fromHex, but uses transparent (`rgba 0 0 0 0`) as default value.

values.name: fromHsl
values.comment:
Create a color from an HSL record

Hue takes a value between 0 and 360.

Saturation and lightness take a value between 0 and 1.
values.type: { hue : Basics.Float, saturation : Basics.Float, lightness : Basics.Float } -> Color.Color

values.name: fromHsla
values.comment:
Create a color from an HSL(a) record

Hue takes a value between 0 and 360.

Saturation, lightness, and alpha take a value between 0 and 1.
values.type: { hue : Basics.Float, saturation : Basics.Float, lightness : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float } -> Color.Color

values.name: fromPalette
values.comment:
Parses different palette formats

    fromPalette "https://huemint.com/website-monochrome/#palette=fffffc-00eb80"
    --> List.filterMap Color.fromHex [ "fffffc", "00eb80" ] : List Color

    fromPalette """
                fffffc
                00eb80
                000000
                """
    --> List.filterMap Color.fromHex [ "fffffc", "00eb80", "000000" ] : List Color

    fromPalette "https://coolors.co/40f99b-61707d"
    --> List.filterMap Color.fromHex [ "40f99b", "61707d" ] : List Color

    -- Infact, it works with everything that has 6 digits hex values
    fromPalette "xxxxfffffc-00eb80x000000---"
    --> List.filterMap Color.fromHex [ "fffffc", "00eb80", "000000" ] : List Color

values.name: fromRgb
values.comment: Create a color from an rgb record. Each channel takes a value between 0 and 1.
values.type: { red : Basics.Float, green : Basics.Float, blue : Basics.Float } -> Color.Color

values.name: fromRgb255
values.comment: Create a color from an rgb record. Each channel takes a value between 0 and 255.
values.type: { red : Basics.Int, green : Basics.Int, blue : Basics.Int } -> Color.Color

values.name: fromRgba
values.comment: Create a color from an rgba record. Each channel takes a value between 0 and 1.
values.type: { red : Basics.Float, green : Basics.Float, blue : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float } -> Color.Color

values.name: gray
values.comment: Create a gray color. I.e. for a 50% gray: `gray 0.5`

values.name: hsl
values.comment:
Provide the hue, saturation and lightness channels for the color.

Hue takes a value between 0 and 360.

Saturation and lightness take a value between 0 and 1.

values.name: hsla
values.comment:
Provide the hue, saturation, lightness, and alpha channels for the color.

Hue takes a value between 0 and 360.

Saturation, lightness, and alpha take a value between 0 and 1.

values.name: invertRgb
values.comment: Invert a color by flipping its r g b values

values.name: isLight
values.comment:
Determine if a color is light, that is, if the lightness is over 50% (`L* > 0.5`). Using the L\* CIELAB color space for human percieved lightness.

Useful for example if you want to change font color based on the lightness of the color.

values.name: lightness
values.comment:
Lightness is the **visual perception** of the luminance L of an object. It is the L\* component of a color in the CIELAB and CIELUV space.

For more information on this, take a look at the implementation details!

values.name: mapHue
values.comment: Map the hue of the color in HSL space

values.name: mapLightness
values.comment:
Map the lightness in the HSL space

    darken : Color -> Color
    darken =
        mapLightness ((*) 0.95)

values.name: mapRgb
values.comment:
Map over rgb channels

    invertRgb : Color -> Color
    invertRgb =
        mapRgb (\c -> 1 - c)

values.name: mapSaturation
values.comment: Map the saturation of the color in HSL space

values.name: rgb
values.comment:
Provide the red, green, and blue channels for the color.

Each channel takes a value between 0 and 1.

values.name: rgb255
values.comment:
Provide the red, green, and blue channels for the color.

Each channel takes a value between 0 and 255.

values.name: rgba
values.comment:
Provide the red, green, blue, and alpha channels for the color.

Each channel takes a value between 0 and 1.

values.name: rgba255
values.comment:
Provide the red, green, blue and alpha channels for the color.

Each channel takes a value between 0 and 255.

values.name: setAlpha
values.comment: Set the alpha of the color

values.name: toCssString
values.comment:
Convert a color to an hex string. Use `toHexString` to omit the `#`-prefix.

    toHexString (rgb 0.5 0.5 0.5) --> "#808080"

values.name: toHexString
values.comment:
Convert a color to an hex string. Use `toCssString` if you want a `#`-prefixed hex value.

    toHexString (rgb 0.5 0.5 0.5) --> "808080"

values.name: toHsla
values.comment: Deconstruct a Color into its hsla channels.
values.type: Color.Color -> { hue : Basics.Float, saturation : Basics.Float, lightness : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float }

values.name: toRgba
values.comment: Deconstruct a Color into its rgba channels.
values.type: Color.Color -> { red : Basics.Float, green : Basics.Float, blue : Basics.Float, alpha : Basics.Float }

values.name: toRgba255
values.comment: Deconstruct a Color into its rgba255 channels.
values.type: Color.Color -> { red : Basics.Int, green : Basics.Int, blue : Basics.Int, alpha : Basics.Int }

values.name: white
values.comment: #FFFFFF

name: Color.Accessibility

unions.name: TextSize
unions.comment:
Text size categories that affect contrast requirements.

  - `Normal`: Regular text size (requires higher contrast ratios)
  - `Large`: Large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold, requires lower contrast ratios)

unions.name: WcagLevel
unions.comment:
WCAG compliance levels for accessibility standards.

  - `AA`: Standard level (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
  - `AAA`: Enhanced level (7:1 for normal text, 4.5:1 for large text)
  - `NotCompliant`: Does not meet any WCAG contrast requirements

values.name: contrastRatio
values.comment:
Calculate the contrast ratio between two colors according to WCAG guidelines.

The contrast ratio ranges from 1:1 (no contrast) to 21:1 (maximum contrast).
It's calculated using the formula: (L1 + 0.05) / (L2 + 0.05) where L1 is the
lighter color's relative luminance and L2 is the darker color's relative luminance.

For semi-transparent colors, they are first blended with the background color
before calculating the contrast ratio, as per WCAG guidelines.

    contrastRatio (rgb 0 0 0) (rgb 1 1 1) --> 21.0 (black on white)

    contrastRatio (rgb 0.5 0.5 0.5) (rgb 1 1 1) --> ~3.98 (gray on white)

    contrastRatio (rgba 0 0 0 0.5) (rgb 1 1 1) --> ~3.98 (50% black blended with white)

values.name: formatContrastRatio
values.comment:
Format a contrast ratio as a string with ratio notation.

        formatContrastRatio 4.5 --> "4.5:1"
        formatContrastRatio 21.0 --> "21:1"

values.name: meetsWcagAA
values.comment:
Check if two colors meet WCAG AA contrast requirements for a given text size.

        meetsWcagAA Normal (rgb 0 0 0) (rgb 1 1 1) --> True (21:1 > 4.5:1)
        meetsWcagAA Normal (rgb 0.7 0.7 0.7) (rgb 1 1 1) --> False (~2.8:1 < 4.5:1)
        meetsWcagAA Large (rgb 0.7 0.7 0.7) (rgb 1 1 1) --> True (~2.8:1 > 3:1)

values.name: meetsWcagAAA
values.comment:
Check if two colors meet WCAG AAA contrast requirements for a given text size.

        meetsWcagAAA Normal (rgb 0 0 0) (rgb 1 1 1) --> True (21:1 > 7:1)
        meetsWcagAAA Normal (rgb 0.7 0.7 0.7) (rgb 1 1 1) --> False (~2.8:1 < 7:1)
        meetsWcagAAA Large (rgb 0.3 0.3 0.3) (rgb 1 1 1) --> True (~5.7:1 > 4.5:1)

values.name: wcagLevel
values.type: Color.Internal.Color -> Color.Internal.Color -> { normal : Color.Accessibility.WcagLevel, large : Color.Accessibility.WcagLevel }
